Row.Text: Turn-by-turn graphs, civilization rankings, global relations, war history, and Triumph progress for every met civilization.
Track each civilization across the course of the game.
Composite power score, the same number the Victory screen uses.
Total number of cities and towns owned.
Number of fully-researched technology nodes in the current age tree.
Number of fully-unlocked civic nodes in the current age tree.
Estimated economic output based on per-turn yields and turns elapsed.
Science per turn — drives technology research.
Culture per turn — drives civics progress.
Net food output per turn across all settlements.
Summary of current diplomatic standing across met civilizations and suzerain city-states.
Scaled real-world population, exponentially derived from total citizen count.
Net diplomatic Influence generated per turn.
Active diplomatic actions, endeavors, sanctions, and espionage involving this leader.
Number of active trade routes leaving this player's cities.
Total territory owned in square kilometers, scaled from controlled hex tiles.
Percentage of the settlement cap this player has used. 100% means at the limit.
How close the current age is to ending. Same for all civilizations.
Current crisis stage (0 = none, 3 = peak crisis). Game-wide value.
Summed combat strength across all of this player's military units (land, naval, and air formations).
Total wonders constructed by this player across all ages.
This civilization is still unmet. Enable "Show names of unmet civilizations and city-states" in Options to show real names.
This city-state is still unmet. Enable "Show names of unmet civilizations and city-states" in Options to show real names.
Show names of unmet civilizations and city-states
When off, unmet civilizations and city-states appear as generic placeholders in charts, the factbook, and global relations.
No civilization has made progress yet.
No samples yet — play a turn and reopen.
Chart failed to render — see UI.log.
No resource samples yet — once you assign your first resource the chart will populate.
Current age: {1_Age}. Cells show triggered / total available; bar is filled percent.
No wars yet. Once any civ declares war, the timeline will populate.
No wars match the current filters.
No samples yet — play a turn first.
CSV is too large ({1_SizeMB} MB). Lower the history cap in Options and try again.
Copied · {1_Rows} rows × {2_Cols} cols{3_SizeTag} · paste into Excel / Sheets / a .csv file
Clipboard export unavailable. CSV written to UI.log.
A single chart cannot span Antiquity, Exploration, and Modern. History resets at each age transition, so charts only show data from the current age. Use Current Age or a year-range filter instead.

An estimated economic output score based on per-turn yields and turns elapsed.

It is a display metric for comparing civilizations over time. It does not affect game state.

A summary of current diplomatic relationships with met civilizations and suzerain city-states.

Higher values reflect stronger relations. Lower values reflect hostility or war. This is a display metric only.
No snapshot yet — the age hasn't ended.
Show all civs at full opacity
Toggle X-axis time units between turn number, in-game year, or both
Hide wonder-built markers on chart lines
Show wonder-built markers on chart lines (icon at the turn each civ completed a wonder)
Copies all sampled turns for all civilizations to the clipboard as CSV. Paste into Excel, Sheets, or another spreadsheet tool to save or analyze the data.
Copy all sampled history as CSV
Tip: click a civilization header to hide it and focus the comparison. Click it again in the right column to restore it.
Triumph Radar — all civs, all 6 attribute paths
Conflicts Timeline — every war this game has seen
Show real names for civilizations I haven't met (spoiler)
Colorblind mode (high-contrast palette for charts and relations)
Show eliminated civs in chart and factbook
Show wonder markers on chart lines
Unlimited ⚠ very large saves, may slow performance
Current effective cap: {1_Cap} · Game speed detected: {2_Speed} · Hard ceiling: {3_Ceiling}
Higher values record fewer points. The change applies on the next turn, and existing samples are kept.
Disable downsampling and keep every recorded turn after the cap is reached. Best used with a high cap or Unlimited.
Clear all recorded wars? Sample history and other data will be kept.
Ring node = met major civ. Multiple lines between two civs = multiple relationships.
Viewer-civ ↔ city-state relationships. Multiple lines = multiple relationships.
The Coming of the Sea Peoples
The Coming of the Horse Lords
The Crisis of the Third Century
History sample cap {1_Cap} — older samples are downsampled to fit.
History sample cap {1_Cap} — no downsampling yet.
